input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen, specifically in .NET 10. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work anymore.

This is a known change in .NET MAUI. In .NET 9+, the Android `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated and MAUI moved to using edge-to-edge display. The recommended approach changed.

Let me provide a concise answer about the modern approach for handling keyboard/soft input in .NET MAUI on .NET 10.


The main solution is switching from `Resize` to `Pan` mode, which is the new standard for handling soft input in .NET 10, though on Android you can also leverage `WindowInsetsCompat` and `ViewCompat.SetOnApplyWindowInsetsListener` for more granular control over how the keyboard affects your layout. The `WindowInsets` approach is the modern way forward, but for a practical MAUI solution on .NET 10, I should recommend setting `adjustPan` in the manifest since `adjustResize` is deprecated, though handling keyboard insets properly with edge-to-edge enabled is really the more robust path forward.
